---
pagetitle: CV
output:
  html_document:
    fig_width: 1
---

[Download CV as pdf.](files/CV_KULD_Lukas.pdf)

<style>
.col2 {
  columns: 2 200px;
  -webkit-columns: 2 200px;
  -moz-columns: 2 200px;
}
</style>

### Employment

<div class="col2">
**University of Limerick**

- Lecturer, Department of Economics, Kemmy Business School
- Since 2022
- Limerick, Ireland
</div>

<div class="col2">
**TU Dortmund**

- Postdoctoral Researcher, Chair of Urban, Regional, and International Economics, Department of Economics
- 2017--2021
- Dortmund, Germany
</div>

### Education

<div class="col2">
**Trinity College Dublin**

- Ph.D. Economics
- Dissertation: "Research collaboration and dissemination in economics"
- Advisor: John O'Hagan
- Committee: Christian Fons-Rosen, Carol Newman
- 2013--2018
- Dublin, Ireland
</div>

<div class="col2">
**ENSAE Paris**

- Statistician-Economist (~M.Sc. grande école program)
- 2010--2013
- Paris, France
</div>

<div class="col2">
**University of Mannheim**

- M.Sc. Economics
- B.Sc. Economics (Minor in Mathematics)
- 2011--2013, 2008--2011
- Mannheim, Germany
</div>

### Honours & Awards

<div class="col2">
**Government of Ireland Postgraduate Scholarship**

- Irish Research Council
- 2014--2017
</div>

<div class="col2">
**Department Scholarship**

- Department of Economics, Trinity College Dublin
- 2013--2017
</div>

<div class="col2">
**ENSAE-Mannheim double degree scholarship**

- Franco-German University (UFA-DFH)
- 2010--2013
</div>

### Computer skills & languages

**Statistics:** R (fluent), working knowledge of Stata, SAS, Matlab, Python

**Languages:** English (fluent), French (proficient), German (native)

### Conference presentations

PUCK Bilbao (2014), EWACE Vienna (2015), ACEI Valladolid (2016), RGS Dortmund (2017), JME Le Mans (2017), EEA-ESEM Cologne (2018), EWACE Copenhagen (2019), ACEI Lille (2021)

### References

- Christiane Hellmanzik, Professor of Urban, Regional and International Economics, Technical University of Dortmund, Germany
- John O'Hagan, Professor of Economics (Emeritus), Department of Economics, Trinity College Dublin, Ireland
- Carol Newman, Professor in Economics, Department of Economics, Trinity College Dublin, Ireland
